{"query":"Shein Hong Kong IPO listing status 2026 headquarters relocation China","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1757,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://finance.yahoo.com/markets/stocks/articles/shein-said-move-ahead-preparations-090028044.html","title":"Shein Is Said to Move Ahead With Preparations for Hong Kong IPO","content":"(Bloomberg) -- Shein Group Ltd. is moving ahead with preparatory work for a possible Hong Kong initial public offering, people familiar with the matter said, potentially capping a yearslong effort by the fast-fashion giant to go public.
Shein and its advisers may seek to launch the IPO as soon as in the coming months if the China Securities Regulatory Commission gives its approval, the people said, asking not to be identified discussing private information.
Shein unsuccessfully tried to list in the US and London before switching to Hong Kong last year, all the while seeing its valuation plummet.
Hong Kong's stock market has also fallen this year, by about 6%, but its IPO market is fizzing, with almost $35 billion raised in first-time share sales already.
Shein's initial plan to go public in the US was derailed two years ago amid scrutiny of its supply-chain and labor practices, and London was abandoned as Chinese regulators withheld their approval.
The retailer moved its headquarters to Singapore in 2021, but it remains subject to CSRC oversight because the regulator requires all firms with substantial links to China, even those that aren't incorporated in the country, to clear its review prior to listing anywhere in the world.
Having spent years playing down its Chinese roots and marketing itself as a global company, Shein shifted tack after applying for the Hong Kong IPO last year.","date":"8 days ago"},{"url":"https://danelfin.com/ipo/shein","title":"Shein IPO Date, Valuation & How to Invest | Danelfin","content":"The company uses on-demand manufacturing and a mobile-first platform, backed by General Atlantic, Sequoia, and Tiger Global.
The Shein IPO has become a test case for where fast-fashion meets geopolitics.
After regulatory friction derailed a US listing, Shein is now pursuing a London or Hong Kong venue, with a valuation reportedly in the $30 to 50 billion range.
Shein is reportedly pursuing a public listing in 2026, with a London or Hong Kong venue under consideration after regulatory hurdles delayed earlier plans.
The company had previously filed confidentially for a US listing, which stalled amid scrutiny from regulators and lawmakers.
Shein has raised capital from investors including Sequoia Capital China (now HongShan), Tiger Global, General Atlantic, and Mubadala, the sovereign wealth fund of Abu Dhabi.
For investors weighing Shein stock and the company's pre-IPO valuation of roughly 30 to 50 billion dollars, relevant publicly listed comparables include fast-fashion operators such as H&M and Inditex, alongside e-commerce platforms like PDD Holdings, the parent of Temu."},{"url":"https://www.reuters.com/world/shein-wins-china-approval-ipo-hong-kong-2026-07-10/","title":"Shein finally wins China's approval for Hong Kong IPO, in ...","date":"8 days ago","content":"8 days ago — Shein won approval. After New York rejected it and London spooked at it, Shein finally got a regulator to say yes. Hong Kong isn't the exit anyone wanted in 2022 — it's the exit that's actually available. Shein cleared Chinese regulatory approval on July 10, 2026 to list in Hong Kong, targeting September or October 2026 and aiming to raise roughly $3B at a $40-50B valuation. That's less than half its $100B peak in 2022, following failed New York (2023-2024) and London (2025) attempts, but it locks in the first real liquidity event for backers like Sequoia China, Tiger Global, General Atlantic, and IDG Capital. Shein cleared Chinese regulatory approval on July 10, 2026 for a Hong Kong IPO — its third attempt at going public, and the first one likely to actually happen. The company is targeting September or October 2026 in Hong Kong, aiming to raise roughly $3B at a $40-50B valuation.","score":0.8364614,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-07-10/shein-gets-approval-from-chinese-regulator-for-hong-kong-ipo","title":"Shein Gets Approval From Chinese Regulator for Hong Kong IPO","content":"### Bloomberg. Shein Global Holdings Ltd. got approval from the Chinese regulator to move ahead with its planned initial public offering in Hong Kong, capping a yearslong effort by the fast-fashion giant to go public. Shein plans to sell up to 341.6 million H shares in the Hong Kong IPO, according to a statement from the China Securities Regulatory Commission Friday. Bloomberg News earlier reported that the company and its advisers were moving ahead with preparations for the listing after recent discussions with the regulator had yielded more positive signals.","score":0.7593865,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.reuters.com/world/shein-wins-china-approval-ipo-hong-kong-2026-07-10","title":"Shein finally wins China's approval for Hong Kong IPO, in ...","content":"Exclusive news, data and analytics for financial market professionals Learn more about Refinitiv. ## Browse World. ## Browse Business. # Shein finally wins China's approval for Hong Kong IPO, in third attempt to go public. LONDON/HONG KONG, July 10 (Reuters) - Fast-fashion retailer Shein won approval for its long-awaited Hong Kong IPO on Friday, a notice posted on the China Securities Regulatory Commission (CSRC) ​website showed, clearing the way for a listing after failed attempts in New York and London. Founded by Chinese-born entrepreneur Sky Xu in 2012, Shein has waited a year for the green light from Beijing for its IPO, which had to be cleared by the highest levels of the ruling Communist Party, according to a source with direct knowledge of the matter. Beijing views Shein as politically sensitive and has been cautious about endorsing a listing after controversies including a sex doll scandal in France ​and reports of poor labour practices at its supplier factories in China, the source said.","score":0.7576693,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.theguardian.com/business/2025/aug/19/shein-mulls-china-return-for-hong-kong-listing-after-london-ipo-stalls","title":"Shein looks at moving back to China for Hong Kong listing after London IPO stalls | Shein | The Guardian","content":"Shein moved its headquarters to Singapore in 2022. # Shein looks at moving back to China for Hong Kong listing after London IPO stalls. The online fashion retailer Shein is understood to be considering moving its base back to China from Singapore to pave the way for a Hong Kong listing. The business, which was founded in China and moved its headquarters to Singapore in 2022, had been considering a £50bn float in London after failing to win approval from regulators in the US for a New York flotation. However, Chinese authorities have not given the nod to a UK listing and the fast-fashion group has faced questions about conditions in its supply chain from campaigners, influential British MPs and investors. The latest effort to secure a listing comes as prospects for growth at the company have been dampened by new restrictions on the import of low-value goods to the US.","score":0.63615024,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.wsj.com/business/retail/shein-hong-kong-ipo-40-billion-6f414bbb","title":"Shein Targets Over $40 Billion Valuation After China Nod ...","content":"This copy is for your personal, non-commercial use only. Dow Jones Reprints at 1-800-843-0008 or visit www.djreprints.com. https://www.wsj.com/business/retail/shein-hong-kong-ipo-40-billion-6f414bbb. # Shein Targets Over $40 Billion Valuation After China Nod for IPO. ## The fast-fashion retailer could list in Hong Kong as early as the third quarter of this year. Fast-fashion retailer Shein has cleared a key hurdle for its long-awaited Hong Kong initial public offering, winning China’s nod for a listing that people familiar with the matter said could see it valued at more than $40 billion.
